notes: SHIP v1.14.213 — BOOT PLATE SWAP (asset-only + required SW bump; per BUILD-SHEET-2026-07-08.md RELEASE 2). Replaced the portrait boot-hero plate.webp with an upscaled 2048×3070 q82 (467KB) file (was 1024×1536, 365KB) — the fallback served on portrait phones was badly upscaled on DPR-3 iPhones (device-soft landing; diagnosed live: 1024-wide native vs ~1179+ device px). Same filename → ZERO HTML markup change; the only dct-ios.html edit is the version constant. plate.webp IS in the sw.js precache list, so the SW CACHE_VERSION key was bumped .212→.213 — WITHOUT that bump devices keep serving the OLD cached 1024 plate and the swap looks like a no-op (this is the one gotcha, NOT a pure no-code swap). version.json + dct-ios.html version constant bumped too to keep three-stamp lockstep even though HTML content is otherwise untouched. plate-wide.webp (landscape, 1672×941) LEFT AS-IS this pass — milder issue, revisit if owner sends landscape source art. Boot-plate srcset idea (from a separate web-Claude session) is CANCELLED per the build sheet — single high-res plate + object-fit:cover is enough; no srcset added, no edits pulled from that session (remote HEAD confirmed == my HEAD, no collision). RECONCILE NOTE: the build sheet's Releases 1/3/4/5/6 (.204 bug batch, Phase Checklist, Log-note chips, Active Strip, Logo-Home) were ALREADY SHIPPED on my line as .204/.212/.208/.209/.211/.210 — only Release 2 (this plate swap) was outstanding; the staged .203-based dct-ios.html was NOT used (live is .212, would have been a blind-overwrite regression). Gates: node --check sw.js + 4 inline HTML blocks · CSS braces unchanged · CRLF preserved · new plate is valid VP8 2048×3070 · three-stamp lockstep .212→.213. ROLLBACK: git revert (restores 1024 plate + old SW key). SHIP-ORDER NOTE: first ship of a NEW batch (prior batch .202-.212 device-verified 'all good' + RELEASED 2026-07-08). DEVICE-VERIFY: hard-refresh / remove+re-add the PWA (URL cache-bust does NOT bypass a registered SW — must let the new SW activate) → boot hero is visibly CRISPER on iPhone. If it looks unchanged, the SW cache-key bump didn't take — check that first (app version should read v1.14.213).
